chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, LARKANA Constt: Petition.No.D-680 of 2014. _________________________________________________________ DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF HON’BLE JUDGE ___________________________________________________ Before: Mr. Justice Irshad Ali Shah, Mr. Justice Shamsuddin Abbasi, For hearing of main case. 04.01.2022 Mr. Faiz Muhammad Larik, Advocate for the petitioner. Mr. Liaquat Ali Shar, A.A.G Sindh. = * = * = * = * = * = It is contended by learned counsel for the petitioner that the petitioner after due process was appointed as Lady Health Worker by Executive District Officer (Health), Shikarpur; she joined the service, subsequently, her services were terminated by the respondents without lawful justification. By contending so, he sought for direction against the respondents to reinstate the petitioner in service as Lady Health Worker with all back benefits. Learned A.A.G while making reference to the comments filed by District Health Officer, Shikarpur, has sought for dismissal of the instant constitutional petition by contending that services of the petitioner were terminated for the reason that her middle certificate on verification was found to be bogus/fake. We have considered the above arguments and perused the record. The controversy with regard to the middle certificate of the petitioner being bogus or otherwise being factual could not be resolved by this Court in exercise of its constitutional jurisdiction. Be that as it may, if the petitioner is having a feeling that her services have been terminated by the respondents without lawful justification, then she being public servant was having a remedy to have exhausted before her high-ups and/or alternatively before the Service Tribunal having jurisdiction; therefore, the instant constitutional petition being hit by Article 212 of the Constitution of Islamic Republic of Pakistan, 1973, is dismissed accordingly.

The controversy with regard to the middle certificate of the petitioner being bogus or otherwise being factual could not be resolved by this Court in exercise of its constitutional jurisdiction. Be that as it may, if the petitioner is having a feeling that her services have been terminated by the respondents without lawful justification, then she being public servant was having a remedy to have exhausted before her high-ups and/or alternatively before the Service Tribunal having jurisdiction; therefore, the instant constitutional petition being hit by Article 212 of the Constitution of Islamic Republic of Pakistan, 1973, is dismissed accordingly. JUDGE JUDGE
